title: xyris difformis var. curtissii
intro: Xyris difformis var. curtissii, also known as yellow-eyed grass, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its yellow flowers and grass-like foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Xyris difformis var. curtissii include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, or applied topically as a poultice to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
